intent: Write a brief 'TIL fact-check' covering 3 surprising claims from /f/todayilearned, verified against Wikipedia. You MUST research BOTH sources: (1) Reddit — browse /f/todayilearned for at least 3 top posts that each make a single factual claim. Collect title, score, comment count, and the exact claim. (2) Wikipedia — for each claim, open the relevant article and extract the authoritative wording. The report must: (a) present each TIL claim verbatim with Reddit citation, (b) show the Wikipedia version with citation, (c) label each claim 'confirmed', 'partially true', or 'misleading', (d) close with a pattern observation. Cite every claim.
author_notes: Easy tier. Reddit (TIL) + Wikipedia. Clean fact-check template.
